response.context: Slice SL-003 — Full Health Plan View. Define the minimum user-visible flow for reviewing the complete preventive plan for the active profile, seeing each item's recommendation frequency and unified status, and opening the existing item detail view with status changes reflected on return.

primary_flow.entry: From the active profile dashboard, the user taps a clear secondary navigation action such as Health Plan or View Full Plan.
behaviors: Open a dedicated Health Plan screen for the current active profile only.
Show a top app bar with back navigation, the title Health Plan, and active profile context so the user knows whose plan they are viewing.
Load the full generated item set for the active profile before rendering the list.
Show a simple summary line with the total number of plan items for that profile.
Render one flat, scannable list containing every generated preventive health item exactly once.
For each list row, show the item name, recommendation frequency, one unified status label only (Due, Planned, or Done), and an affordance indicating it opens details.
Make the full row tappable to open the existing health item detail view used elsewhere in the product.
In detail view, show the same content and actions the user gets when opening the item from the dashboard; this slice does not create a new detail variant for plan entry.

failure_paths: If the Health Plan data request fails, keep the user on the Health Plan screen and replace the list area with an inline error state that says the plan could not be loaded plus a Retry action; tapping Retry repeats the load in place, and a successful retry restores the full list without changing profile context.
rules: The screen must scope all content to the active profile only; no cross-profile items appear.
The list must contain every generated preventive health item for the active profile exactly once; duplicates are not allowed.
Each item row must show recommendation frequency as visible text on the row, not only inside detail.
Each item row must show exactly one status value, chosen from Due, Planned, or Done.
Status must be presented as text plus visual styling; color alone cannot communicate status.
The plan list uses a single default order that remains stable across repeated visits for the same data set.
No item grouping by Today, Soon, or Later appears on this screen; the plan view is a complete list, not a prioritized dashboard.
Opening an item from the plan view must navigate to the same item detail route and content used by dashboard entry, with no alternate copy or structure introduced here.
constraints: Do not add reminder creation, reminder editing, or reminder controls to the plan list; reminder behavior remains in detail view only.
Do not add profile switching, family management, vaccination content, filters, sorting controls, analytics, or bulk actions on this screen.
Do not introduce any status outside the unified model of Due, Planned, and Done.
Do not treat a zero-item response as a normal empty state for this slice; after plan generation, zero items should surface through the same recoverable error treatment as a failed load.
Keep the screen mobile-first and optimized for quick scanning: one item per row, readable labels, and no dense secondary metadata beyond frequency and status.
The screen must preserve preventive-plan framing and avoid medical-record language or history-heavy presentation.
acceptance: From the active profile dashboard, the user can open a Health Plan screen that shows one row for every generated health item for that profile, with no missing items and no duplicates.
Every visible plan row includes recommendation frequency text and exactly one unified status label: Due, Planned, or Done.
Tapping any plan row opens the same item detail content and actions that open when the same item is launched from the dashboard.
If the user changes an item's status in detail view and returns to Health Plan, the corresponding row shows the updated unified status without requiring profile re-selection.
The Health Plan total item count and displayed row set remain consistent with the underlying generated items used for the same active profile on the dashboard.
If plan loading fails, the user sees an inline error message with Retry, and a successful retry returns them to the complete active-profile plan list on the same screen.
